spec.title: Helper registry capability model and fixture stub

humanSummary.text: Final re-audit found no P0/P1 blockers after sealing fixture helper execution behind HelperRegistry. Earlier public fixture validation and invalid-field coverage gaps remain as non-blocking follow-up work.

findings.title: Public manifest validation does not validate helper-registry payloads
findings.description: The public manifest includes helper-registry fixture entries, but the manifest validator only schema-validates helper-result roles; helper-registry entries currently receive hash and byte checks without AJV payload validation.

evidence.detail: Initial audit observed helper-registry manifest entries and schema, while validate-public-manifests.mjs only validates helper-result payloads.

findings.impact: Public CI can miss helper-registry schema drift even though KAIFUU-086 relies on those fixtures as the public contract for helper capability metadata.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Public manifest validation schema-validates entries with role helper-registry against helper-registry.schema.json.
A negative helper-registry fixture or validator unit test proves malformed helper-registry payloads fail public fixture validation.
Existing helper-result validation behavior remains unchanged.

proposedDagNode.title: Public helper-registry manifest validation
proposedDagNode.summary: Extend public fixture validation so helper-registry fixture payloads are schema-validated, not only hash-checked.
deliverables: AJV validation path for helper-registry manifest entries
Malformed helper-registry validation fixture or targeted validator test
Updated public fixture validation documentation if role handling changes
acceptanceCriteria: fixtures/validate-public-manifests.mjs validates helper-registry entries against helper-registry.schema.json.
Malformed helper-registry payloads fail with a semantic fixture validation error.
Existing helper-result manifest validation still passes.
auditFocus: Helper-registry schema drift hidden by hash-only validation

findings.title: Helper-registry invalid-field coverage is narrower than the contract
findings.description: KAIFUU-086 validates helper id, version, execution policy fields, and allowlist references, but public invalid fixtures focus on missing capability, bad schema id, and unsupported redaction class.

evidence.detail: Initial audit accepted implementation behavior but noted missing negative fixtures for helper id, helper version, execution policy, and allowlist ref id diagnostics.

findings.impact: Future helper-registry changes could weaken validation for explicit registry contract fields without focused fixture coverage catching the drift.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Invalid helper id, helper version, execution policy, and allowlist reference cases have focused tests or public invalid fixtures.
Diagnostics name the offending registry field.

proposedDagNode.title: Helper-registry invalid-field fixture matrix
proposedDagNode.summary: Add focused helper-registry invalid-field fixtures for helper identity, version, execution policy, and allowlist reference validation.
deliverables: Invalid helper id and version fixtures
acceptanceCriteria: Helper-registry invalid-field tests fail on helper id, version, execution policy, and allowlist reference regressions.
Each failure reports the offending field path.
The fixture helper still validates and invokes through the registry boundary.
